summary: Short summary of the family and why it is a meaningful registry entry.
philosophy: Teach the canonical family surface first and keep the escape hatch explicit.
Keep semantics and accessibility rules in core so React, Vue, and Svelte stay aligned.
Use the family API to make product intent self-documenting where possible.

useWhen: When this family is the canonical choice for the interaction or content pattern.
When the family contract should stay aligned across React, Vue, and Svelte through shared core logic.
avoidWhen: When a simpler native element or another family is the more honest semantic choice.
When the team cannot explain the family intent clearly enough to choose a stable API path.

automationCovers: Shared contracts or adapter tests that prove the core behavior.
Storybook docs or taxonomy tests that protect the teaching surface.
manualReviewRequired: The remaining edge cases that automation cannot fully prove.
Any policy decision that still needs a documented accessibility stance.
notes: Keep roadmap anchors for AXE-specific follow-up here, not audit index links.

openQuestions: Only keep live family-level questions here.
